q: The time period of a simple harmonic oscillator is$T=2$\pi  k m . Measured value of mass ( m ) of the object is 10 g with an accuracy of 10 mg and time for 50 oscillations of the spring is found to be 60  s  using a watch of 2  s  resolution. Percentage error in determination of spring constant ( k ) is ________%.
explanation: The formula for the time period of simple harmonic oscillator is$T=2$\pi  k m . ⇒  T$2=( 2 \pi  ) 2 ( k m ) ⇒ k=T$2 ⋅  \t\text{m}^{4}  \pi  2 Using propagation of error, when quantities are multiplied or divided, their relative errors (percentage errors) are added. The constants have no error. \Delta  k k \times$100=( \Delta  m m+2 ⋅ \Delta   T   T  ) \times  100 The measured value of mass is, m=10gA ccuracy/Erro r$in measurement of mass is, \Delta$m=10mg=0.01 g So, percentage error in measurement of mass \Delta  m m \times  100 % = 0.01 10 \times$100=0.1 % The percentage error in the time period (  T  ) is the same as the percentage error in the total time ( t ) measured for n oscillations. Total time measured is,$t=60$s   Resolution/Error  in measurement of time is, \Delta$t=2$s  So, percentage error in measurement of time is, \Delta$t^{t} \times  100 %=2 60 \times  100 %=3.33 % So, using propagation formula: Percentage error in$k=( % error in m )+2 \times  ( % error in t )=0.1 % + 2 \times  3.33 % = 6.76 % Therefore, the correct option is ( D ).
